```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The toppling of the Serra statue provided an opportunity to explore the controversy surrounding Junipero Serra as a symbol of enslavement and genocide for Native Americans.",
    "The mission system created by Junipero Serra held Indigenous people in servitude, forced conversions, and contributed to starvation and disease.",
    "Attempts by Indigenous people to escape the mission system were dealt with harshly.",
    "The impact of the mission system on Indian people has not been told completely or accurately.",
    "Pope Francis acknowledged the 'grave sins' of colonialism against Indigenous people in the Americas during a 2015 trip to Bolivia.",
    "A statue of Junipero Serra on Capitol grounds represents a double injury to the Miwok and Nisenan people.",
    "The statue of Junipero Serra symbolizes an earlier time of genocide, slavery, and other degradations imposed upon California Indians.",
    "AB 338 proposes to remove the requirement for a statue of Junipero Serra on the State Capitol grounds.",
    "AB 338 requires the replacement of the Serra statue with a monument created with the input of local Tribal Nations.",
    "The history of what it took to build the State Capitol has not been fully told or understood."
  ]
}
```
